title: rating.submitted
description: Published by Rating Service when a user submits a rating. Consumed by Rating Service itself (self-consumption pattern) to trigger trust score recalculation asynchronously.
_comment_self_consume: Rating Service both produces and consumes this event. This is a deliberate decoupling of submission from calculation — the write side (accept rating) is separated from the read-side projection (recalculate trust score). Prevents the HTTP request from blocking on recalculation.
_comment_tags_enum: Tags are constrained to a fixed vocabulary here. Adding a new tag means updating this enum — which forces a versioning conversation. Free-text tags would silently diverge between services.

rated_id.description: User whose trust score will be recalculated.

tags.description: Structured feedback tags. Positive and negative tags drive trust score direction. Empty array is valid (neutral rating).
